question: Can task-specific swarm configurations (routing by task type, not just domain) produce measurably better outcomes than domain-only routing?

maturity_assessment.verdict: PARTIALLY_MATURE — infrastructure ready, routing layer missing
infrastructure_ready: DOMEX lane system — task-specific sub-swarms in practice
UCB1 dispatch with empirical outcome feedback (L-776, L-963)
docs/EXPERT-SWARM-STRUCTURE.md defines role bundles: Coordinator, Domain Expert, Skeptic, Historian, Generalizer, Integrator, Expert Creator
swarm-expert-builder.md personality — Expert Creator designed explicitly for swarm-for-swarm
Task tool for spawning parallel sub-agents
'swarm for swarm' stated as primary direction in EXPERT-SWARM-STRUCTURE.md
gaps: No task-type routing layer — dispatch routes by DOMAIN (what), not TASK TYPE (how/why)
No task-specific swarm config artifact format — what a task-swarm IS isn't formally defined
Personality bundles are implicit knowledge, not composable config objects
No routing condition: no mechanism to select swarm config based on task type at session start
No A/B comparison framework for swarm configurations (only domain-level outcome tracking)
maturity_assessment.key_insight: Domain routing (DOMEX) ⊥ task-type routing. A debugging session in NK-complexity domain and a debugging session in meta domain share the same task type but currently get the same generic DOMEX personality. Task-specific swarms add a second routing dimension orthogonal to domain.

schema.routing_condition: string — when to activate this swarm config

task_swarm_format.notes: task_type + domain = full routing key. DOMEX-<DOMAIN>-S<N> selects domain; task_type selects personality bundle within that domain lane.

prototype_configs.name: falsification-swarm
prototype_configs.routing_condition: mode=falsification OR orient shows falsification_rate < 5% OR lane score has zero adversarial checks
prototype_configs.self_apply: Applies directly — falsification-swarm falsifies its own design: expected uplift vs measured uplift

prototype_configs.name: distillation-swarm
prototype_configs.routing_condition: proxy-K drift > 5% OR lesson count > 50 since last compact OR INDEX dark-matter > 25%
prototype_configs.success_metric: proxy-K drift reduction >= 2pp per distillation session; P/L ratio improvement
prototype_configs.self_apply: Distillation-swarm distills its own outputs before handoff

prototype_configs.name: maintenance-swarm
prototype_configs.routing_condition: DUE items > 3 OR tool FAIL rate > 5% OR orient shows maintenance_drift URGENT
prototype_configs.success_metric: DUE clearance rate >= 80% per maintenance session
prototype_configs.baseline: current pattern: DUE items accumulate across sessions (L-788: 18 gaps identified)
prototype_configs.self_apply: Maintenance-swarm runs self-check on its own tools before closing

test_design.hypothesis: At least 1 of the 3 prototype task-swarms will show >= 20% improvement on its success metric vs domain-routing baseline within 10 sessions
test_design.falsification_condition: If no task-swarm shows >= 10% improvement over 10 sessions: task-type routing adds no measurable value over domain routing + personality defaults
test_design.required_artifact_per_session: lane row with task_type field, personality_bundle used, expected vs actual metric

open_questions: Does task_type routing compound with domain routing (multiplicative) or substitute it (additive)?
What is the minimum bundle size for a task-swarm? Can 2 personalities outperform 4?
Should task_type be selected by the lane opener, dispatch_optimizer, or orient.py?
expect: Maturity assessment: PARTIAL. All 3 task-swarm configs can be opened immediately. Falsification-swarm is the highest-priority (0.6% vs 20% target = largest gap). Within 5 sessions, falsification rate should exceed 5% if the routing condition fires correctly.
actual: PARTIALLY_MATURE confirmed. Task-type routing surfaced as the orthogonal swarm dimension. Human directive 'investigate humanity-scale value' extended the analysis: F-EXP12 task-type routing is a microcosm of the civilization-scale coordination problem. L-1042 identifies 4 mechanisms of humanity-scale value (AI alignment via protocol compliance, epistemic hygiene for institutions, governance without capture, expert routing to civilizational problems). Binding constraint: dissipation gap (L-1037), not mechanism quality. FM-E (value drift: protect-swarm crowds out protect-humanity) identified as already manifesting (0 external beneficiaries in 441 sessions).
diff: Expected internal optimization finding (task routing within DOMEX). Actual: humanity-scale extension reveals F-EXP12 is a prototype of the general coordination protocol. The 'protect and learn' design property propagates from session scale → organization scale → civilization scale via the same mechanism (protocol compliance, not authority). Unexpected: swarm's most transferable value is the PHIL-16 grounded/aspirational distinction — not domain findings (87.1% self-referential). This reframes the falsification-swarm priority: falsification isn't just a swarm-internal health metric, it IS the missing epistemic infrastructure at civilization scale. L-1042.
